title: Drafts Restore
description: When paused work is ready to resume, it needs to move back to the active list without losing any lifecycle state or history. `specd drafts restore <name>` recovers a drafted change from `drafts/` back to `changes/`, preserving its state exactly as it was when drafted.
context: When paused work is ready to resume, it needs to move back to the active list without losing any lifecycle state or history. `specd drafts restore <name>` recovers a drafted change from `drafts/` back to `changes/`, preserving its state exactly as it was when drafted.

rules:
```
specd drafts restore <name> [--format text|json|toon]
```

Alias:

```
specd draft restore <name> [--format text|json|toon]
```

- `<name>` — required positional; the name of the drafted change to restore
- `--format` — controls output encoding; defaults to `text`
The command moves the change from `drafts/` to `changes/` and appends a `restored` event to history. The lifecycle state is preserved exactly as it was when the change was drafted.

On success, the command prints a single line to stdout:

```
restored change <name>
```

rules.requirement: Output on success — JSON and toon
rules:
When `--format json` is passed, the command writes a JSON object to stdout on success:

```json
{ "result": "ok", "name": "..." }
```

When `--format toon` is passed, the same data model is written in Token-Oriented Object Notation (toon).

- If no change with the given name exists (in any location), the command exits with code 1 and prints an `error:` message to stderr.
- If the change exists but is not currently in `drafts/` (i.e. it is active or discarded), the command exits with code 1 and prints an `error:` message to stderr.

constraints: Restoring does not affect lifecycle state
A change may be drafted and restored multiple times; each cycle appends events to history
Errors always go to stderr as plain text regardless of --format

scenarios.name: Missing name argument
when: specd drafts restore is run without a name
then: the command exits with code 1 and prints a usage error to stderr

scenarios.name: Singular alias invocation
when: specd draft restore old-experiment is run
then: it is accepted as an alias of specd drafts restore old-experiment

scenarios.name: Change moved back to active
given: a drafted change old-experiment in drafts/ with lifecycle state designing
when: specd drafts restore old-experiment is run
then: the change is moved to changes/
AND a restored event is appended to history
AND the lifecycle state remains designing

scenarios.name: Success message — text
given: a drafted change old-experiment in drafts/
then: AND the process exits with code 0

scenarios.requirement: Output on success — JSON and toon
scenarios.name: Success message — JSON
given: a drafted change old-experiment in drafts/
when: specd drafts restore old-experiment --format json succeeds
then: stdout is a JSON object with result set to "ok" and name set to "old-experiment"
AND the process exits with code 0

scenarios.name: Change not found
when: specd drafts restore nonexistent is run and no change named nonexistent exists anywhere
then: AND stderr contains an error: message

scenarios.name: Change not in drafts
given: my-change is active in changes/ (not drafted)
when: specd drafts restore my-change is run
then: AND stderr contains an error: message
